summary: possibly Gaucher's disease, but more likely a mis-oriented interpretation
detail: This genotype technically represents carrying two copies of the D409H mutation in the GBA gene, and thus most likely a form of Gaucher's disease. However, errors in interpretation for C/G transversion mutatations occur far more frequently than Gaucher's disease does, so this is likely to actually be an error.
